items.headline: India begins first caste census in 94 years as part of nationwide population count
items.body: India launched its census on April 1, counting 1.4 billion people and for the first time since 1931 enumerating caste categories beyond scheduled groups. The data will reshape political representation and welfare targeting across the subcontinent for decades.

items.headline: Cyclone flooding drives cholera cases above 200,000 across six southern African countries
items.body: Mozambique, Malawi, Zimbabwe, Zambia, Tanzania, and South Africa report surging cholera infections following Cyclone Filipo's March flooding, with treatment centres overwhelmed. The outbreak threatens regional food security as farmers cannot access fields during planting season.

items.headline: Philippines extends energy emergency as power rationing enters fifth week
items.body: Rotating blackouts continue across Luzon affecting 60 million people after the government extended emergency powers through April to address coal and natural gas shortages. Manufacturing output has contracted as factories operate partial shifts and hospitals rely on backup generators.

items.headline: France deploys additional troops to New Caledonia after independence referendum violence
items.body: Paris sent 300 more security personnel to the Pacific territory this week following clashes between pro-independence groups and loyalists ahead of a May self-determination vote. The deployment signals France's concern about losing its largest exclusive economic zone and Indo-Pacific strategic position.

es.headline: EU member states approve Gibraltar border removal deal but delay implementation three months
es.body: All 27 EU members backed the post-Brexit treaty eliminating passport checks between Spain and Gibraltar. The border removal now scheduled for mid-July will end daily queues for 15,000 cross-border workers.

no.headline: Norway fuel tax cut fails to lower pump prices as energy companies capture most of reduction
no.body: Road fuel taxes were eliminated April 1, cutting levies by over four kroner per liter for petrol and nearly three for diesel. Pump prices fell only a fraction of the tax reduction, with companies appearing to retain margins while oil crisis drives consumers toward electric vehicles.

uk.headline: Britain launches Fair Work Agency with powers to investigate employers without worker complaints
uk.body: The new enforcement body consolidates minimum wage, holiday pay, and sick pay oversight under single authority with 550 inspectors. Employers face proactive inspections and penalties up to 200% of underpayments as fragmented enforcement system ends.

nl.headline: War-driven fuel costs push Dutch households toward heat pumps and solar installations
nl.body: Iran conflict triggered demand surge for renewable energy systems as installers report being overwhelmed by inquiries. The shift marks acceleration toward household energy independence driven by crisis rather than climate policy alone.

nl.headline: PostNL abandons next-day mail delivery standard after decade of volume decline
nl.body: National postal service extends delivery to 48 hours starting July as letter volumes dropped 70 percent since 2009. The change restructures routes for thousands of workers and ends universal rapid postal service.
